The '''RHTC-560 Hunter Trainer''' was a training/[[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] system developed by [[Rodian D-Tec]] to help track and eliminate prey during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]].

==Description==
===Master Control Unit===
[[File:RHTC560diagram-FF86.png|thumb|left|250px|An RHTC-560 technical diagram]]
The RHTC-560 system consisted of one bipedal control unit and up to ten smaller [[HT drone]]s. The master controller unit was bipedal in shape and form; resembling both its [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] creators and an [[Armor/Legends|armored]] [[Spider/Legends|arachnid]].<ref name="EGD" /> Towering at a height of 1.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] and armed with sharp claws on both their hands and feet, these [[Droid/Legends|droids]] excelled in their role as [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]]/training droids. The RHTC-560 master unit's ferocity in combat was further reinforced by its speed and agility. Its advanced programming also gave it access to some of the latest stealth and evasion techniques available in its time. While typically unarmed, some were even modified to carry two forearm-mounted [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]]. 

The 560 was also the tactical brain for the smaller HT drones which it used for tracking its hunters and defending itself, coordinating their tactics and cooperation, and recording their video input.<Ref name="guide">''[[Shadows of the Empire Planets Guide]]''</ref> 

Since it was also required to interact with its masters, the controller unit was equipped with a [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulator]], a wideband [[communications transceiver]], and a [[Sensor array/Legends|sensor array]] system. Meanwhile, a [[Scomp link/Legends|scomp link]] access port allowed the droid to pilot the drones through any obstacle as well as to display maps and replay recordings of its and their activities. These were essential for the debriefing and evaluation of the trainee hunters following a hunt.<Ref name="guide" /> 

==History==
[[File:RHTC560.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A RHTC-560 Hunter Trainer control droid fighting [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] on the ''[[Anya Karu]]'']]
The RHTC-560 Hunter Trainer system was originally marketed locally by [[Rodian D-Tec]] on the Rodian [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]] to suit the native Rodian appetite for violence and combat. Their customers ranged from [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], to the various Rodian clans which used them in both their [[Rodian Clan Wars|clan wars]] and hunts, to even the dictatorial [[Grand Protector/Legends|Grand Protector]] [[Navik/Legends|Navik the Red]]. It was alleged that the Grand Protector's RHTC-560 system had been modified with lethal [[Disruptor/Legends|disruptor]] cannons for used against his captives in competitions.

Throughout the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], their popularity was sufficient enough for the series to be marketed offworld beyond the confines of Rodia. With 5,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for each control unit and another 2,000 credits for each HT drone, the series was economical enough for many non-Rodian bounty hunters, as well as local [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] militias and police academies. Three were also given to the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the ''[[Anya Karu]]'', [[Aron Harcourt]]. They would later fight, and lose to, the bounty hunter [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]].
